content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence AAA of NNN integers such that every element is a non-negative power of 222.

A sequence is called `good` if its sum is a non-negative power of 222. You would like to turn AAA into a `good` sequence.

To achieve this, you can perform the following operation on AAA:


Pick a non-empty subsequence of AAA, pick a positive integer XXX such that X‚â§230X \leq 2^{30}X‚â§230, and multiply every element of this subsequence by XXX.
Find the minimum number of operations required to turn AAA into a good sequence. Also, find one sequence of operations which does this. If there are multiple possible answers, you may find any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print the answer in the following format:



First, print one line containing an integer MMM, denoting the minimum number of moves required.
Then, print 2M2M2M lines describing MMM operations.

Each operation is described by 222 lines.
On the first line, print two space-separated integers KKK and XXX, denoting the size of the subsequence and the multiplier for this operation.
On the second line, print KKK distinct space-separated integers denoting the indices of the elements chosen to be multiplied in this operation. These KKK integers can be printed in any order.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Multiplying the 1st,2nd1^{st}, 2^{nd}1st,2nd and 3rd3^{rd}3rd elements by 222 turns the array into [8,16,8,32][8, 16, 8, 32][8,16,8,32], whose sum is 64=2664 = 2^664=26.
Test case 222: The array is already good.
